A generous vertical gap introduces the main two-column body: on the left, a taller, compact column houses the FAQ search and question list; on the right, a wider column presents the currently active policy text in long-form editorial style. Near the top of this right column, a subtle inline 'Review cart' button sits slightly offset from the text, acting as a secondary focal element. The entire composition maintains wide side margins and generous spacing, so policy details and FAQs feel calm rather than cluttered. At the very bottom, breaking out of the two-column rhythm, a full-width, centered 'Stay updated' CTA anchors the page, visually acting like a footer call-to-action while still floating in ample whitespace.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page controller wraps all content inside a centered max-width container (~960–1140px) with wide side margins. It positions the page’s local title or intro (if present) at the top center, occupying a single column similar to the artist name area in the mockup. This top block is given generous vertical margins—about 48–64px above and below—so it visually floats in whitespace. Below this controller header, the tab navigation is placed with a modest 24–32px gap, maintaining a continuous vertical flow. The controller also defines the main content band starting roughly one quarter down the viewport, where the two-column layout (FAQ on the left, active policy content on the right) begins. It manages which markdown section is visible based on tab selection, ensuring only one policy content section appears in the right column at a time while FAQ components and CTAs remain consistently placed.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as the structural container and orchestrator for child components. It is centered, scrolls with the page (no fixed positioning), and establishes the global vertical rhythm and two-column grid for the inner components on larger screens. On smaller screens, it collapses columns into a single centered column while preserving vertical ordering.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The tab navigation is centered horizontally directly beneath the page controller’s heading area, in a single row that spans roughly 60–70% of the container width. Each tab label is evenly spaced with ample left/right padding, and the entire bar has light visual weight—thin typography, subtle underline or bottom-border for the active tab. A 24–32px gap above separates it from the page controller heading, and a larger 48px gap below separates it from the two-column content area, mirroring the mockup’s generous white space between navigation and main content. On narrower screens, the tabs remain horizontally scrollable or wrap to two lines but are still centered within the container.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, non-sticky by default, participating in the normal document flow. On larger screens, it is anchored at the center of the page; on mobile, it maintains full-width alignment but keeps ample horizontal padding to retain the airy look.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When the Shipping tab is active, this markdown section occupies the right column of the main two-column layout. It starts approximately 48px below the tab navigation baseline, aligning vertically with the top of the FAQ column on the left. The section spans about 60–65% of the container width and is positioned flush with the right edge of the main content grid, leaving a 24–32px gutter between it and the FAQ column. Inside, the heading and paragraphs follow an editorial style: the section title sits near the top-left of this column with generous spacing above, and text flows downward in well-spaced paragraphs. Near the top-right of this column, vertically aligned with the first or second line of body text, the inline 'Review cart' CTA is placed slightly offset from the paragraph flow, echoing the mockup’s CV download link position. Below this, the content continues vertically with comfortable line height and 16–24px spacing between subsections. On mobile, this section shifts to full-width, appearing directly below the FAQ search and list, with the review-cart CTA still near the top of the text block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Displayed only when the Shipping tab is selected; otherwise hidden or collapsed. It participates in the right column of the responsive two-column grid on desktop, and becomes a standard full-width block in the single-column mobile layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Returns section mirrors the Shipping section’s layout when its tab is active. It takes over the same right-column area, starting at the same vertical level as the FAQ column, occupying about 60–65% of the container width aligned to the right side. The section heading appears at the top-left of this column with ample whitespace, and the body text flows downward with 16–24px spacing between paragraphs. The inline 'Review cart' CTA is again positioned near the top-right corner of this column, visually aligned with the first block of text, so users scanning the Returns content see it immediately as a secondary focal point. On mobile, the Returns content renders in the same full-width editorial style below the FAQ area, maintaining consistent spacing and typographic rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Visible exclusively when the Returns tab is selected; otherwise hidden. It uses the same right-column slot within the two-column layout on larger screens and behaves as a full-width stacked section on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Care section follows the same spatial pattern as other policy markdown sections: it occupies the right-column band beneath the tabs, taking 60–65% of the container width and aligning to the right with a consistent gutter to the left FAQ column. The Care heading anchors the top of this block, with text arranged in well-separated paragraphs that feel like a clean editorial article. As with the other sections, the 'Review cart' CTA appears near the top-right of this column, offset slightly from the main text to draw attention without adding visual heaviness. The rest of the Care content flows down with clear subhead spacing (24–32px gaps) to maintain scanability. On mobile, the Care content becomes a full-width text block that appears in the same vertical position relative to the FAQ area as the other sections do when active.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Only rendered when the Care tab is active. It shares the right-column slot on desktop, and converts to a single-column stacked section in the mobile layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Privacy section also shares the unified right-column layout convention. When the Privacy tab is active, this section fills the right-hand column beneath the tabs, starting at the same height as the FAQ column. It uses approximately 60–65% of the container width, with a consistent gutter separating it from the left column. The Privacy heading sits near the top-left of the block, and the content flows downward with generous line-height and 16–24px spacing between paragraphs and lists to keep potentially dense text readable. The 'Review cart' CTA remains in its familiar spot near the top-right edge of the column, aligning with the first segment of text to maintain a predictable, quiet hierarchy across all policy sections. On smaller screens, this section is presented as a full-width content block, stacked after the FAQ elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Visible only when the Privacy tab is selected. It uses the same right-column slot in the two-column grid on desktop and reflows to a full-width block in the mobile single-column layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ search form is placed in the upper portion of the left column within the main content band. This left column occupies roughly 35–40% of the container width, aligned to the left edge of the content grid, with a 24–32px gutter separating it from the policy text column on the right. The search input itself is centered within this left column horizontally, spanning about 80–90% of its width, with 16–24px padding around it to preserve the minimalist, airy feel. There is a generous 48px gap above between the bottom of the tab navigation and the top of the search box, mirroring the vertical break between sections in the original mockup. Below the search bar, a 24px gap leads into the FAQ list, keeping the search visually connected but not cramped.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Always visible regardless of which tab is selected (or visible specifically when the FAQ-related tab is chosen, depending on behavior), anchored at the top of the left column in the two-column grid. On mobile, it becomes a full-width element that appears above the FAQ list and policy section content, maintaining its role as the starting point for FAQ exploration.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ list is stacked directly below the FAQ search form in the left column, filling the remaining vertical space in a continuous, scrollable block. It uses the full width of the left column, with each FAQ item separated by 16–24px of vertical spacing so questions and answers are easy to scan, similar to short editorial snippets. The list’s top edge begins about 24px below the search form, and its vertical extent typically parallels or slightly exceeds the height of the right-column policy content, creating a balanced two-column composition reminiscent of the portrait-and-text pairing in the mockup. On smaller screens, the FAQ list follows directly under the search form in a full-width stack, appearing above the active policy text section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in the left side of the two-column grid on larger screens and converts into a full-width block directly beneath the search form on mobile. It scrolls naturally with the page; no sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'Stay updated' CTA is positioned beneath the main two-column region, occupying a full-width row within the centered container. There is a substantial vertical gap (48–64px) between the bottom of the longest column (either FAQ list or policy text) and this CTA, allowing it to sit in its own spacious band, similar to a quiet footer call-to-action. The button itself is centered horizontally, with generous horizontal padding and minimal visual embellishment—thin type, clear outline or subtle fill—to remain consistent with the minimalist aesthetic. Additional fine print or supporting text (if any) appears just above or below the button, aligned center, with ample line spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element in the vertical flow, spanning the container width but visually centered. It sits at the bottom of the info page content and shifts naturally into a full-width centered button in the single-column mobile view.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'Review cart' inline CTA is placed inside the right-column policy content area as a slender, high-visibility but low-weight control. It sits near the top of whichever policy markdown section is currently active—typically aligned with the first heading or first paragraph—offset slightly to the right so that it appears visually distinct from the main text flow, similar to the blue CV download link in the mockup. On desktop, it aligns with the right edge of the policy column, with 8–16px spacing from the top text block and at least 16px of padding inside the button or pill shape. This makes it easy to spot at the initial glance without competing with the page heading. On mobile, it remains near the top of the policy text block but spans only as wide as needed for its label, centered or right-aligned within the full-width text area to preserve its inline, secondary nature.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: This CTA is tied contextually to the currently visible policy markdown section and moves with that section within the right column. It does not float or stick; instead, it participates in the text flow but is visually offset to behave like a secondary focal point early in the reading sequence.
